metadata.title: LSC 6.2.0: A Phenomenological Framework for Neutrino Propagation and Detector-Frame Tensor Anisotropy
metadata.description:
LSC 6.2.0 is the latest preprint-stage continuation of the public LSC 6.0 phenomenological framework. It introduces a detector-frame tensor anisotropy formulation that separates propagation-level true energy from detector-level reconstructed energy. The draft is prepared for arXiv submission and is currently awaiting category endorsement.

The model is not presented as confirmed new physics. It is a testable phenomenological ansatz requiring independent review, numerical fitting, and constraints from BEST/GALLEX/SAGE gallium source data, KATRIN beta-spectrum measurements, IceCube anisotropy/systematic studies, and standard three-flavor oscillation data.

This record is intended to preserve continuity of the LSC research line for grant review and independent verification.

On 2026-04-30 the 6.2.2 correction layer was added as a DOI-preserving extension note: the isotropic trace was made explicit, the directional detector term remained traceless, the mixed detector `1 / E^2` usage was removed from the base ansatz, and deterministic simulations were added for trace / anisotropy separation. The original DOI remains the citable anchor for the record.
metadata.notes: arXiv submission is prepared but awaiting category endorsement. This Zenodo package is intended as the stable citable preprint record while arXiv endorsement is pending. The 2026-04-30 6.2.2 correction is recorded as a DOI-preserving extension note rather than a new record.
